address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: Forty-one short pieces by the Báb, titled "بیان" (Bayán) at the head of the first piece, which Browne calls the Shu'ún-i-Khamsa ("Five Grades" or "Stations"). Excluding the last piece (an apparent appendix or peroration), the remaining forty fall into eight groups of five, each group beginning with a shared formula; within each group of five, Browne infers the first piece corresponds to the "first grade" or "style" of the Báb's writings, the second to the second grade, and so on - the fifth piece of each group being, consistently, in Persian (corresponding to the "fifth grade"). Browne characterizes many of these pieces, especially those consisting of permutations of the divine "Names," as "not only untranslateable, but almost unreadable."

msItem.title: Shuʼūn khamsah
شؤون خمسه
Shu'ún-i-Khamsa (The Five Grades)
msItem.note: Browne explains the Báb's own account of the "five grades" or styles of his writing (citing Persian Bayán, Váḥid 6, ch. 1, and Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel's own gloss): the first resembles previous revealed scripture, the second is supplication and prayer, the third is exhortation, the fourth is scientific treatises and commentaries and answers to enquirers, and the fifth restates the same content in Persian.
msItem.incipit: بسم‌الله الله لاله انی انا الله لا اله الا انا الاله الاله... (no. 1, f. 2b)
msItem.explicit: شهد الله انه لا اله لا هو العزيز المحبوب... (no. 41, f. 198a)

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBF. 2 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts (Continuation)", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

msItem.textLang: Arabic, with the fifth piece of each group of five in Persian

handDesc.handNote: Written in Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel's clear and legible naskh hand.

history.origin: Place and date of transcription not stated by Browne.
history.provenance: Accompanied Ṣubḥ-i- Ezel's second letter to Browne, despatched from Famagusta, Cyprus, 1 October, received 11 October 1889.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. ", abbreviated by the OCIMCO project team; provenance expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábí Manuscripts," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Jul. 1892, pp. 462-470.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBF_2.xml) into this Fihrist record: summary, item notes, incipit/explicit samples, physical description, hand note, and provenance history rewritten from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 462-470). Arabic quotations are unproofed OCR (Tesseract), flagged cert="low". Person/place authority keys reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel, Cyprus). Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Julian Cook Changed person and work ids (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Removed origDate tags around something which is not a date using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
